author: @ThisRandomUsername
text: I didn't buy the kit, but you should put up the .pdf on Dropbox as public and link it in the description. It'd be useful for people to print out the diagram and have it ready on the day.

author: @TheRealMrLaserCutter
text: I nominate James biggar, a YouTuber who makes DIY opensource renewables like windturbines and heat pumps.

replies.author: @TheRealMrLaserCutter
replies.text: Oops I posted this in the wrong video, it was ment for the supercap or battery video about looking for potential reviewers

author: @dotoid
text: Hi there i have a question. I believe you have made videos on this method before ones with sodium sulfate and another with sulfuric acid and sodium hydroxide as electrolyte. And in both videos you used ~10v one switching between +- and one not. So is nacl and 12V just used because its easier to get hold of for the demonstration or is it working better? I guess there is room for experimentation.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: spot on mate - we are using that because it is safe and there is a ton of room for experimentation

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: somewhere between 2 and 10 minutes - that is something to experiment with

author: @chrissweet2309
text: Hi Robert, my name is Christopher sweet I had bought your kit for the battery making live event but had missed the live event due to family emergency I am wondering is there anyway you can send me a link so that I have the recorded live event. My email is csweet4242@yahoo.com I would greatly appreciate any help you could give, as it was not my intention to miss that awesome event dot-dot-dot but as you well know life happens. I appreciate the time and hopefully I will hear from you soon

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can find it here mate - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9ygN7Pz1qcc - as you say life happens but it is a little harsh sometimes lol - hope this helps - cheers
